content.question: Can a government retain administrative discretion to deny selective-track expansion (as demonstrated in the church-school case) without triggering legal challenge from private/church operators, or will such denials face judicial review that constrains further administrative action?
content.reduction_path: Obtain legal analysis of ministerial discretion limits under Hungarian education law and obtain records of any court challenges to prior expansion-denial decisions; consult education ministry legal counsel and church-school association litigation posture.
